member.name: M:Microsoft.DotNet.Cli.CommonOptions.CreateInteractiveOption(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: A 'template' for interactive usage across the whole dotnet CLI. Use this as a base and then specialize it for your use cases. Despite being a 'forwarded option' there is no default forwarding configured, so if you want forwarding you can add it on a per-command basis.
member.param: Whether the option accepts an boolean argument. If false, the option will be a flag.
member.remarks: If not set by a user, this will default to true if the user is not in a CI environment as detected by . If this is set to function as a flag, then there is no simple user-provided way to circumvent the behavior.

member.name: M:Microsoft.DotNet.Cli.CommonOptions.CreateNoLogoOption(System.Boolean,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Creates an implementation of the --no-logo option. This option suppresses the display of the startup banner or logos for commands or products it is applied to. The behavior of this option is influenced by the DOTNET_NOLOGO environment variable if it is set:
If the --no-logo option is not specified on the command line, the environment variable will be checked to determine whether to suppress logos. Any truthy value - true, 1, yes, on - will suppress logos, while any falsy value - false, 0, no, off - will show logos.
If the option is specified on the command line, it takes precedence over the environment variable.
Finally, if neither the option nor the environment variable is set, the option will default to the provided .

member.summary: Set up an option to be forwarded as an output path to MSBuild
member.param: The command line option
The property name for the output path (such as OutputPath or PublishDir)
Whether the path should be surrounded with double quotes. This may not be necessary but preserves the previous behavior of "dotnet test"

member.name: M:Microsoft.DotNet.Cli.OptionExtensions.ForwardAsMSBuildProperty(System.CommandLine.Option{System.Collections.ObjectModel.ReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.String}})
member.summary: Set up an option to be forwarded as an MSBuild property This will parse the values as MSBuild properties and forward them in the format optionName:key=value. For example, if the option is named "--property", and the values are "A=B" and "C=D", it will be forwarded as: --property:A=B --property:C=D. This is useful for options that can take multiple key-value pairs, such as --property.

member.name: P:Microsoft.DotNet.Cli.CommandDefinitionStrings.CmdBlameDescription

Runs the tests in blame mode. This option is helpful in isolating problematic tests that cause the test host to crash or hang, but it does not create a memory dump by default.
When a crash is detected, it creates an sequence file in TestResults/guid/guid_ ...
